diagrams.description: Physical Anthropology is the scientific study of human biological evolution, variation, and adaptation. This flowchart illustrates its systematic approach: the ultimate goal is to understand human origins and biological diversity. It integrates core subfields (human evolution and paleoanthropology, primatology, human variation and adaptation, bioarchaeology and forensic anthropology) with foundational theoretical frameworks (evolutionary theory, population genetics, natural selection). These are investigated through key methods (comparative anatomy, fossil analysis, genetic and genomic tools, morphometrics) that reveal patterns of phylogenetic descent, adaptation to environments, and modern human variation. The insights produced inform fields like medicine, forensic identification, and our understanding of human uniqueness.
